report_metadata.purpose: Deep-dive into BitNet/ternary mathematics for mask-locked inference chip optimization

ternary_MAC_definition.mathematical_form: y = Σᵢ wᵢ × xᵢ where wᵢ ∈ {-1, 0, +1}
ternary_MAC_definition.critical_insight: Multiplication is eliminated — becomes conditional addition/subtraction/skip

eliminated_operations.ternary_replacement: 2-bit control signal directly routes to ALU

BitLinear_layer.description: Replaces standard nn.Linear with ternary weights + INT8 activations
operations_sequence: 2. AbsMax quantization of activations to INT8

key_equations.activation_quantization: x̃ = Clip(Round(x/|x_max| × Q_b), -Q_b, Q_b) where Q_b = 127 for INT8

popcount_optimization.description: For binary operations, popcount replaces addition
popcount_optimization.formula: For binary inputs x ∈ {0,1}, ternary weights: y = popcount(x∧w_pos) - popcount(x∧w_neg)
popcount_optimization.hardware_benefit: Single-cycle accumulation using population count circuits
popcount_optimization.applicability: Limited for INT8 activations, but useful for certain sparse patterns

ternary_MAC_unit.description: No multiplier — only adder-subtractor with 2-bit weight control
ternary_MAC_unit.savings: 38-43% fewer gates, 60-65% smaller area, 2× faster timing

mask_locked_MAC_unit.description: Weight encoded in metal — no weight register or decode
mask_locked_MAC_unit.additional_benefit: Zero weight memory access, zero switching for zero-weights
mask_locked_MAC_unit.power_savings: 80-90% lower dynamic power vs traditional MAC

ternary_pipeline.note: Can be further pipelined for higher throughput

mask_locked_pipeline.unique_benefit: Weight is always 'available' — no fetch, no decode

systolic_array_implementation.description: Ternary weights enable massive systolic arrays without weight registers
systolic_array_implementation.area_efficiency: 2-3× more PEs per mm² vs traditional systolic arrays

weight_stationary_optimization.power_benefit: No weight movement = zero weight-related power
weight_stationary_optimization.unique_property: Weight access time is literally zero (it's a wire)

opportunities_not_possible_in_software.name: Spatial Weight Distribution
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.description: Weights etched as physical wires in metal layers
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.benefit: Zero runtime memory access for weights
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.mathematical_implication: W @ x becomes pure combinational logic (weighted sum of inputs)
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.hardware_technique: Metal routing encodes weight sign (+1/-1/0) as connection topology

opportunities_not_possible_in_software.name: Zero-Weight Gaps
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.description: Physical absence of connection for w=0
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.benefit: Zero switching activity for that weight
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.mathematical_implication: Sparsity is 'baked in' — no need for sparse matrix libraries
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.hardware_technique: Simply don't route that connection — 0×x is physically absent

opportunities_not_possible_in_software.name: Pre-computed Weight Statistics
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.description: Model weights are known at design time
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.benefit: All weight-dependent constants can be precomputed
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.mathematical_implication: These become hardwired constants, not runtime divisions

opportunities_not_possible_in_software.name: Fixed Point Optimization
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.description: No dynamic range concerns from weight updates
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.mathematical_implication: Bit widths for accumulators can be mathematically proven sufficient
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.hardware_technique: Integer-only datapath with guaranteed no overflow

opportunities_not_possible_in_software.name: Matrix Structure Exploitation
opportunities_not_possible_in_software.description: W is known, can analyze structure at design time
examples: Identify weight patterns for custom datapaths
Optimal dataflow graph compiled into hardware

layer_norm_constants.description: LayerNorm requires mean(μ) and variance(σ²) per token, but scaling can be precomputed
layer_norm_constants.traditional: y = (x - μ) / σ × γ + β, where γ, β are learned parameters
layer_norm_constants.mask_locked: γ, β can be baked into subsequent layer weights
layer_norm_constants.hardware_benefit: Eliminates per-layer constant storage, reduces multiplications

attention_scaling.mask_locked: Scale factor absorbed into Q projection weights during compilation
attention_scaling.hardware_benefit: One less multiplication per attention score

accumulation_bounds.calculation: For n weights, max |Σwᵢxᵢ| ≤ n × max|xᵢ|
accumulation_bounds.hardware_benefit: Precise accumulator sizing, no guard bands needed

weight_distribution_analysis.implication: ~33% of weights are zero → 33% of MACs consume zero power
weight_distribution_analysis.mask_locked_advantage: This is physical sparsity — wires literally don't exist

structured_pruning_opportunity.description: Design-time analysis can identify rows/columns with high zero density
structured_pruning_opportunity.technique: Permute weight matrix to cluster zeros together
structured_pruning_opportunity.hardware_benefit: Entire compute units can be power-gated for zero-heavy regions
structured_pruning_opportunity.not_possible_in_software: Software must still check each weight; hardware can be designed to skip regions

optimized_algorithm.description: Decompose W = W⁺ - W⁻ where W⁺, W⁻ are binary matrices
optimized_algorithm.hardware_technique: Parallel W⁺ and W⁻ computation, single subtraction

popcount_based.formula: For x ∈ {0,1}: W @ x = popcount(W⁺ ∧ x) - popcount(W⁻ ∧ x)
popcount_based.hardware_benefit: AND gates + popcount units → extremely efficient
popcount_based.speedup_potential: 10-100× over traditional MAC for binary inputs

int8_activations.multiplication: Ternary weight × INT8 = conditional add/sub of INT8

ternary_activations.hardware: XNOR + NOT gates (XNOR for product, NOT for sign)
ternary_activations.research_note: Ternary activations work for CNNs but not transformers

research_findings: INT8 activations: Negligible accuracy loss (<1% on MMLU)
INT4 activations: ~2-3% degradation, may be acceptable for edge
INT2 activations: ~5-10% degradation, requires careful QAT
Ternary activations: ~15-25% degradation, not recommended for LLMs
optimal_activation_bit_width.recommendation: INT8 for production, INT4 as option for ultra-low-power

mask_locked_optimizations.description: γ, β absorbed into adjacent layer weights
mask_locked_optimizations.hardware: Eliminates 2 multiplications and 2 additions per element

mask_locked_optimizations.description: 1/√σ² can use lookup table with limited precision
E[x²] - E[x]² identity for streaming computation
mask_locked_optimizations.hardware: Two running sums instead of storing all values

ternary_friendly_approximations.name: Shifted Softmax
ternary_friendly_approximations.description: Subtract max before exp to prevent overflow

ternary_friendly_approximations.name: Log-Softmax
ternary_friendly_approximations.description: Compute in log domain, use addition instead of multiplication

ternary_friendly_approximations.name: Fixed-point Softmax
ternary_friendly_approximations.description: Pre-compute exp LUT for INT8 range
ternary_friendly_approximations.hardware: 256-entry ROM for exp lookup, 8-bit adder for sum

mask_locked_advantage.description: Attention dimension d_k is fixed at design time
mask_locked_advantage.implication: Softmax input range is bounded, LUT can be precisely sized

operations: QKᵀ: Matrix-matrix multiply (ternary weights for Q,K projections)
Scale: Divide by √d_k (precomputed constant for mask-locked)
×V: Matrix-matrix multiply (ternary weights for V projection)
mask_locked_specific: Q, K, V projection weights are ternary
Attention pattern (after softmax) is dynamic, computed per-token
KV cache: Mutable INT8, stored in SRAM

walsh_hadamard_transform.description: Ternary matrices related to Hadamard matrices
walsh_hadamard_transform.property: Hadamard matrices have entries ±1, subset of ternary matrices
walsh_hadamard_transform.fast_algorithm: WHT in O(n log n) time
walsh_hadamard_transform.relevance: If weight matrices have Hadamard-like structure, can exploit fast transforms

grouped_accumulation.description: Group weights by value before accumulation
algorithm: 1. Group all +1 weights: sum₊ = Σ xᵢ for all i where wᵢ = +1
2. Group all -1 weights: sum₋ = Σ xⱼ for all j where wⱼ = -1
grouped_accumulation.benefit: No conditional operations in inner loop

binary_decomposition.description: Represent W as difference of two binary matrices
binary_decomposition.formula: W = P - N where P, N ∈ {0,1}
hardware_implementation: Compute P @ x and N @ x in parallel
binary_decomposition.benefit: Binary matrix-vector multiply is very efficient (AND + popcount)

bitnet_b158_performance.conclusion: Ternary weights achieve parity with FP16 at 2B scale

research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.question: Can we design a softmax approximation optimized for ternary weights?
research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.potential_approach: Learned temperature scaling, piecewise linear exp
research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.impact: Could eliminate all multipliers from attention

research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.question: What accuracy degradation for INT4 activations with ternary weights?
research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.impact: 50% reduction in activation memory and adder size

research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.question: Can we train models with structured zero patterns?
research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.potential_approach: Sparse training with block sparsity constraints

research_gaps_and_investigation_needed.question: What is the minimum safe accumulator bit-width?

references.citation: Wang et al., 'BitNet b1.58: The Era of 1-bit LLMs', Microsoft Research, 2024
references.key_contribution: Established ternary weight quantization for LLMs

references.citation: Ma et al., 'BitNet b1.58 2B4T Technical Report', 2025
references.key_contribution: First open-source native 1-bit LLM at 2B scale

references.citation: Liu et al., 'TeLLMe: A Low-Bit Quantization Framework for LLMs on FPGA', 2024
references.key_contribution: First FPGA implementation of ternary LLM

references.citation: Chen et al., 'FATNN: Fast and Accurate Ternary Neural Networks', ICCV 2021
references.key_contribution: Efficient ternary inference on various hardware

references.citation: Hardware-friendly AI algorithms: Ternary Neural Networks, HAL Archives, 2021
references.key_contribution: Comprehensive analysis of ternary NN hardware implementations

references.citation: Power-Efficient Implementation of Ternary Neural Networks, Universidad de Granada, 2022
Design implementations of ternary logic systems, ScienceDirect, 2024
A Logic-Compatible eDRAM Compute-In-Memory for Neural Networks, IEEE, 2021
references.key_contribution: Ternary eDRAM cell for in-memory computing
